sections.paragraph:
Μαρκίων, ωνος, ὁ Marcion, a rare name (Sb 4604, 3).

① a Christian of Smyrna MPol 20:1 (Μάρκου is also attested, as well as the form Μαρκιανοῦ, which is in the Lat. version and is preferred by Lghtf.; s. on this OvGebhardt, ZWT18, 1875, 370ff).

② the famous heretic EpilMosq 3 (Just., A I, 26, 5; 58, 1).—AvHarnack, Marcion2 1924; MEnslin, The Pontic Mouse: ATR 27, ’45, 1–16.—ABD IV 514–20.

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Lat. Lat. = Latin

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

EpilMosq EpilMosq = Epilogus Mosquensis—List 1

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

ATR ATR = Anglican Theological Review—List 6

ABD ABD = Anchor Bible Dictionary—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 617
